paper_title: Economic Growth and Complexity in the UAE: Summary Report
research_answer: Service exports have been the standout performer in the UAE's non-oil export story. Between 2005 and 2019, service exports grew at 9.7% per year — outpacing non-oil goods export growth of 7.7% per year — and by 2019 had reached roughly USD 70 billion, 3.5 times their 2005 value. Crucially, services maintained steady and consistent growth throughout the entire period, while non-oil goods exports stagnated after 2011–2012. Even within the 7.7% goods figure, much of the growth was concentrated in stone exports and some chemicals and metals — largely in the 2005–2012 window — meaning domestically produced, non-re-export goods have been essentially flat for most of the period.
supporting_quotes: From 2005 to 2019, non-oil goods exports grew at a brisk 7.7% per year.
Non-oil service exports have grown even stronger during this time at 9.7% per year, growing by a factor of 3.5.
In 2019, according to the WTO, the UAE's service exports stood at around USD 70 billion (3.5 times the value in 2005).
Non-oil non-re-exports are still stagnant overall. On the other hand, services exports have seen steady and consistent growth during the entire period.
